title: Escaped identifiers with periods emits bad VCD output
body:
---


Author Name: **Trevor Williams** (@phase1geo)
Original Redmine Issue: 107 from https://www.veripool.org
Original Date: 2009-08-05
Original Assignee: Wilson Snyder (@wsnyder)

---

Escaped identifiers that contain periods within the name emit bad output.  A small example that generates the bad output is provided in the attached files.  The command to compile and run is:

verilator --cc --trace --exe test.cc test.v; cd obj_dir; make -f Vtest.mk; ./Vtest

The VCD output looks like the following:

```
$version Generated by SpTraceVcd $end
$date Tue Aug  4 23:20:20 2009
 $end
$timescale 1ns $end

$scope module TOP $end
  $var wire  1 $ verilatorclock $end
  $scope module v $end
    $var wire  1 $ verilatorclock $end
     $scope module a $end
     $scope module b $end
      $var wire  1 # c $end
     $upscope $end
    $upscope $end
  $upscope $end
 $upscope $end
$enddefinitions $end


#0
0#
0$
#1
1$
#2
0$
#3
1$

```

As you can see, the escaped identifier is being blasted into non-existent scopes in the VCD output.  The integrity of the signal needs to be retained in the dumpfile.

Let me know if you need any additional information.

Thanks,
Trevor Williams
